channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Threatens Painful Strikes if US Renews Attacks as Hormuz Standoff Continues
item.description: Iran warned it would respond with long and painful strikes on US positions if Washington renews attacks, while also restating its claim to the Strait of Hormuz, complicating US efforts to build a coalition to reopen the critical waterway.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine to Seek Clarification From US on Russian Ceasefire Proposal
item.description: Ukraine will seek clarification from the Trump administration on details of a Russian proposal for a brief ceasefire next week, President Zelenskiy announced as diplomatic efforts continue.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Ukraine Sees Path to Japanese Arms After Tokyo Eases Export Rules
item.description: Japan's relaxation of weapons export rules opens a path for talks that could lead to Tokyo supplying military equipment to help Ukraine resist Russia's invasion, Kyiv's ambassador to Japan told Reuters.

item.title: ⚔️ Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Oil Refinery 1,500 km From Frontlines
item.description: Ukraine's SBU security service said its drones struck an oil refinery deep inside Russia near Perm, marking a second consecutive attack on energy facilities as Kyiv seeks to curb Moscow's war funding.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Hits Four-Year High Above on Iran War Escalation Fears
item.description: Global oil prices eased after hitting a four-year high of more than per barrel on concerns the US-Iran conflict could lead to prolonged Middle East supply disruption with deeper global economic damage.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Yen Steadies After Japan's Currency Intervention
item.description: The yen trimmed gains against the dollar following Tokyo's first currency intervention in nearly two years, but remains on track for its strongest weekly rally in over two months. Japan warned speculators it stands ready to act again.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 Trump Considers Troop Withdrawals from Italy and Spain
item.description: President Trump said "probably" when asked about pulling U.S. troops from Italy and Spain, one day after announcing potential reductions in Germany. European allies face uncertainty over America's NATO commitment.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German Chancellor Merz Stresses Transatlantic Partnership
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz emphasized the importance of the U.S.-Germany partnership hours after Trump signaled potential troop reductions. Germany faces pressure to boost its own defense spending amid shifting American priorities.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇨🇳⚔️ Pakistan commissions first Chinese attack submarine in naval milestone
item.description: Pakistan commissioned its first Hangor-class attack submarine in a ceremony in Sanya, attended by the president and naval chief, marking a major defense cooperation milestone between the two countries.

item.title: 🇮🇷💥⚔️ Iran digging out buried missiles in preparation for truce collapse
item.description: Iran is preparing for potential ceasefire collapse with the US and Israel by excavating buried missiles and weapons, according to reports from regional intelligence sources.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸💻⚠️ Chinas data reach sparks US espionage and sabotage concerns
item.description: Washington accuses Beijing of harvesting key American data through apps and connected vehicles, weaponizing it as a strategic asset amid growing espionage concerns.

item.title: 🛢️🌍🚢 Strait of Hormuz critical for global food system, not just oil
item.description: Experts warn that the Strait of Hormuz is about much more than oil and gas — it is essential for keeping the global food system operating, with serious implications if the waterway remains blocked.

item.title: 🇲🇲 Myanmar Junta Transfers Aung San Suu Kyi to House Arrest
item.description: Myanmar's military government has moved deposed leader Aung San Suu Kyi to house arrest in the capital, five years after her detention following the 2021 coup. Her legal team plans to meet with her this weekend.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Intervenes to Support Yen as Oil-Driven Selloff Intensifies
item.description: Japan's Ministry of Finance confirmed it intervened to support the yen against the US dollar after USD/JPY crashed over 500 pips. The move comes as oil prices spike due to Iran war concerns, weakening the yen.

item.title: 🛢️ UAE OPEC Exit May Not Crude Oil Prices as Expected
item.description: The UAE's withdrawal from OPEC is seen as lessening the producer group's clout, but analysts say it may not result in sharply lower crude prices as initially anticipated. The exit marks a seismic shift in global oil geopolitics.

item.title: 🇩🇪 US Troops in Germany Highlight Strategic Value as Trump Mulls Pullback
item.description: Senior US officers highlighted the benefits of American presence in Germany, a day after Trump said he was reviewing whether to reduce troop numbers. The timing underscores growing tensions in transatlantic relations.

item.title: 🇺🇸⚖️ US Official Says Iran Ceasefire Terminated Hostilities for War Powers Deadline
item.description: A senior Trump administration official declared that the US-Iran ceasefire that began in early April has terminated hostilities for the purposes of the congressional war powers deadline, which passed at midnight Thursday.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 Trump Criticizes German Chancellor Merz, Tells Him to Stop Interfering on Iran
item.description: President Trump attacked German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, saying he was "doing a terrible job" and should spend less time interfering with Washingtons effort to tackle the Iran nuclear threat.

item.title: 🇮🇹🚢 Italy Condemns Israel Interception of Gaza Aid Flotilla as Unlawful
item.description: Italys government condemned Israels seizure of aid ships bound for Gaza and demanded the immediate release of all Italians who have been unlawfully detained, Prime Minister Giorgia Melonis office said.

item.title: 🇪🇺🌎 EU-Mercosur Trade Deal Takes Provisional Effect After Years of Negotiations
item.description: The long-awaited trade deal between the South American bloc Mercosur and the European Union has taken provisional effect, marking one of the largest trade agreements ever concluded.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 China and US Economic Chiefs Hold Candid Talks Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Top US and Chinese economic officials held candid talks ahead of a meeting set for May between President Trump and President Xi Jinping, with both sides raising complaints about trade policies.

item.title: 🇦🇺🌾 Australia's Farmers Hit by Fertilizer Crisis as Iran War Chokes Hormuz Trade
item.description: Australian farmers face soaring fertilizer costs and supply disruptions as the Iran-US conflict blocks shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, threatening crop yields and livestock production.

item.title: 🇧🇷 Brazil Congress Slashes Bolsonaro's Prison Term in Major Blow to Lula
item.description: Brazil's Congress overrode President Lula's veto, dramatically reducing former president Bolsonaro's sentence from 27 years to just over 2 years, with lawmakers chanting 'freedom' in celebration.

item.title: 🇮🇶🇺🇸 Trump Invites Iraq's PM-Designate to Washington After Productive Call
item.description: President Trump spoke with Iraq's newly designated Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi, inviting him to visit Washington and hailing a 'tremendous new chapter' in US-Iraq relations.

item.title: 🌍 China Eliminates Tariffs for All But One African Nation
item.description: Beijing's new zero-tariff regime covers nearly all African countries, boosting Chinese soft power on the continent but raising questions about uneven economic benefits and resource extraction dynamics.
